1	\paragraph{1993 A--1 \textnormal{[\texttt{integral-evaluation}]}}
2	The horizontal line $y=c$ intersects the curve $y = 2x - 3x^3$
3	in the first quadrant as in the figure. Find $c$ so that the areas of the
4	two shaded regions are equal. [Figure not included. The first region is
5	bounded by the $y$-axis, the line $y=c$ and the curve; the other lies
6	under the curve and above the line $y=c$ between their two points of
7	intersection.]
8

file.content:
\paragraph{1993 A--1 \textnormal{[\texttt{integral-evaluation}]}}
The horizontal line $y=c$ intersects the curve $y = 2x - 3x^3$
in the first quadrant as in the figure. Find $c$ so that the areas of the
two shaded regions are equal. [Figure not included. The first region is
bounded by the $y$-axis, the line $y=c$ and the curve; the other lies
under the curve and above the line $y=c$ between their two points of
intersection.]

input.content:
import Mathlib

open scoped BigOperators

noncomputable def putnam_1993_a1_solution : ℝ := 4 / 9

/-- Antiderivative of `4/9 - (2x - 3x^3)`. -/
noncomputable def F1993 (x : ℝ) : ℝ := 4 / 9 * x - x ^ 2 + 3 / 4 * x ^ 4

theorem putnam_1993_a1 :
    0 < putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
    putnam_1993_a1_solution < 4 * Real.sqrt 2 / 9 ∧
    ∃ x1 x2 : ℝ, 0 < x1 ∧ x1 < x2 ∧
      2 * x1 - 3 * x1 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      2 * x2 - 3 * x2 ^ 3 = putnam_1993_a1_solution ∧
      (∫ x in (0:ℝ)..x1, (putnam_1993_a1_solution - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))
        = ∫ x in x1..x2, ((2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3) - putnam_1993_a1_solution) := by
  unfold putnam_1993_a1_solution
  -- derivative of the antiderivative
  have hderiv : ∀ x : ℝ, HasDerivAt F1993 ((4:ℝ) / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)) x := by
    intro x
    unfold F1993
    have e1 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => (4:ℝ) / 9 * y) ((4:ℝ) / 9) x := by
      simpa using (hasDerivAt_id x).const_mul ((4:ℝ) / 9)
    have e2 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => y ^ 2) (2 * x) x := by
      have h := hasDerivAt_pow 2 x
      convert h using 1
      norm_num
    have e3 : HasDerivAt (fun y : ℝ => (3:ℝ) / 4 * y ^ 4) (3 * x ^ 3) x := by
      have h := (hasDerivAt_pow 4 x).const_mul ((3:ℝ) / 4)
      convert h using 1
      norm_num
      ring
    have hsum := (e1.sub e2).add e3
    convert hsum using 1
    ring
  -- continuity of the integrand
  have hcont : Continuous (fun x : ℝ => (4:ℝ) / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)) := by fun_prop
  refine ⟨by norm_num, ?_, (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, 2 / 3, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- 4/9 < 4√2/9
    nlinarith [Real.sq_sqrt (show (0:ℝ) ≤ 2 by norm_num), Real.sqrt_nonneg 2]
  · -- 0 < (√3 - 1)/3
    nlinarith [Real.sq_sqrt (show (0:ℝ) ≤ 3 by norm_num), Real.sqrt_nonneg 3]
  · -- (√3 - 1)/3 < 2/3
    nlinarith [Real.sq_sqrt (show (0:ℝ) ≤ 3 by norm_num), Real.sqrt_nonneg 3]
  · -- curve hits c = 4/9 at x1
    have hs : Real.sqrt 3 ^ 2 = 3 := Real.sq_sqrt (by norm_num)
    linear_combination (-(Real.sqrt 3 - 3) / 9) * hs
  · -- curve hits c = 4/9 at x2 = 2/3
    norm_num
  · -- the two shaded areas are equal
    have hL : (∫ x in (0:ℝ)..(Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3, ((4:ℝ) / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))
        = F1993 ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3) - F1993 0 :=
      intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt (fun x _ => hderiv x)
        (hcont.intervalIntegrable _ _)
    have hR : (∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ), ((4:ℝ) / 9 - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3)))
        = F1993 (2 / 3) - F1993 ((Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3) :=
      intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt (fun x _ => hderiv x)
        (hcont.intervalIntegrable _ _)
    have hcongr : (∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ), ((2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3) - (4:ℝ) / 9))
        = ∫ x in (Real.sqrt 3 - 1) / 3..(2 / 3 : ℝ), (-(((4:ℝ) / 9) - (2 * x - 3 * x ^ 3))) := by
      apply intervalIntegral.integral_congr
      intro x _
      ring
    rw [hL, hcongr, intervalIntegral.integral_neg, hR]
    have h0 : F1993 0 = 0 := by norm_num [F1993]
    have hd : F1993 (2 / 3) = 0 := by norm_num [F1993]
    rw [h0, hd]
    ring
